---
title: "Maëlle Delouis"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/profile.png
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:delouis-jost@soziologie.uzh.ch"
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=arPECroAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate"
    - icon: person-badge
      text: ORCID
      href: "https://orcid.org/0009-0009-9388-176X"
    - icon: chat
      text: Bluesky
      href: "https://bsky.app/profile/maelledelouis.bsky.social"
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: "https://it.linkedin.com/in/maëlle-delouis-jost-9a4271197"
---

**PhD Student, Department of Sociology, University of Zurich**

I am a PhD student in the Department of Sociology at the University of Zurich, with research interests at the intersection of sociology and political science.

My work examines how state institutions shape national identity and the decision to naturalise. I study conscription and national belonging in reunified Germany, the effect of abolishing conscription on naturalisation among second-generation immigrants in the Netherlands and Sweden, and naturalisation as a response to Switzerland's anti-Muslim referenda.

In parallel projects, I examine how administrative capacity shapes naturalisation take-up in German districts, the long-run effects of conscription on immigration attitudes across eleven European countries, and the electoral consequences of the Tour de France in French municipalities. Methodologically, I rely on causal inference designs (regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences, and counterfactual estimators) applied to population registers, panel surveys, and electoral data.

[Download CV](assets/delouis-cv.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm role="button"}
